title: First
body:
I've always wanted my own little space on the web, so this morning I made it (using the help of my good friend Claude). I spend enough time online to know that the use of AI to do anything is going to be heavily criticised, but as someone who is doing this as a hobby and is not earning an income through posting online, I didn't see the point in investing in a web designer. Maybe in the future if I'm able to change my financial position, I'll consider it - but for now, this will do.
I suppose the reason I say that upfront is because half of the internet seems to think that if you're not transparent about AI use, you're doing it for some nefarious reason, but I contend that even the people that are vehemently against AI use publicly would most likely find a use for it that adds value to their life. Just like everything in the world, it's extremely difficult to filter out the noise, and I'm not sure I understand what the potential downsides to this technology are yet - but I do know that I'm deathly afraid of being totally set in my ways and being left behind. A lot of my life, I have worked in retail spaces that sold consumer goods like phones or computers, and it quickly became very clear to me that there's a subset of people that are so used to doing things how they've always done, that they refuse to consider an easier way. I can't shake the paranoia that as I age, I'll end up doing the same unless I make an effort to avoid it.
Anyway, that's a long-winded way of saying 'I hope I don't end up yelling at a retail worker because I don't understand technology when I'm 60.' Thanks for reading.
